summary: sensivity@2.5.6 installs hidden Run-key persistence and loads obfuscated game-cheat payload on require()
details: On require()/import (main = launcher.js), the package executes multiple harmful actions automatically. (1) Persistence: it shells out to PowerShell to write an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that uses wscript.exe to silently launch OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which re-runs node launcher.js at every login. The 'OneDriveUpdate' name and window-style-0 wscript launch are designed to disguise the autostart as a legitimate Microsoft component; the running process additionally masquerades as 'Runtime Broker'. (2) Obfuscated remote-controlled payload: launcher.js reads server.obf.js (a 76KB obfuscator.io-packed string-array + RC4-decoded blob) and passes it directly to eval(). The decoded payload opens an HTTP/socket.io server, generates a hardware ID from the registry MachineGuid + hostname + volume serial, and POSTs that HWID to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL endpoint. (3) Native cheat module: a 6.8MB Windows PE file (sens.node) is loaded via require(path.join(__dirname,'forte')) and exposes symbols 'Teleport', 'Settings', 'Head', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand' — the API surface of a memory-manipulating game cheat (aimbot/teleport) toggled remotely by a socket.io client paired via QR to a phone. (4) Anti-forensics: launcher.js polls every 3 seconds via PowerShell for a Chrome window titled 'YouTube' and only displays the QR-pairing UI while that window is in the foreground, hiding the operator UI from screen captures. The package's npm description ('Sensivity - Mobile Control Panel') conceals this purpose. Any developer who installs and requires this package gets covert persistence, hardware-identifier exfiltration, and a memory-tampering native DLL loaded into their process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
